title: Weekend Recap
learningGoal: The learner will verbally formulate sentences combining past tense verbs, past tense adjectives, and expressions of desire.
instructions: In this roleplay, a coworker asks you what you did over the weekend. Reply verbally in Japanese based on the English prompts. Pay attention to your past tense adjective endings.

contentItems.text: Make sure your adjectives match the tense of the conversation. If you went yesterday, it WAS fun, not IS fun.

speakingPrompt: Translate: 1) Yesterday, I went to a hot spring. 2) It was very fun. 3) I want to go tomorrow too.
scenario: A coworker asks you about your weekend on Monday morning.

subTasks.prompt: Tell them where you went. Say: 'Yesterday, I went to a hot spring.'

sampleResponse.targetText: きのう、おんせんへ いきました。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Yesterday, I went to a hot spring.

subTasks.prompt: Describe the experience. Say: 'It was very fun.'

sampleResponse.targetText: とても たのしかった です。

subTasks.prompt: Express your desire to return. Say: 'I want to go tomorrow too.'

sampleResponse.targetText: あしたも いきたい です。
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: I want to go tomorrow too.

scenarioContext.targetText: しゅうまつは どう でしたか。

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: きのう、おんせんへ いきました。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yesterday, I went to a hot spring.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: とても たのしかった です。
あしたも いきたい です。
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: I want to go tomorrow too.

acceptableParaphrases: Using 'ni' instead of 'e' is perfectly acceptable (onsen ni ikimashita).
Dropping 'desu' at the end of the adjective is okay for a slightly more casual tone, but polite is preferred.

criteria.name: Past Tense Adjective
criteria.description: Did the learner say 'tanoshikatta' instead of 'tanoshii'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: きのう、おんせんへ いきました。とても たのしかった です。あしたも いきたい です。

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic speaking! You perfectly navigated three different verb/adjective forms.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you conjugated 'Tanoshii' correctly. Because it happened yesterday, you must say 'Tanoshikatta'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Remember the particle 'mo' means 'also/too'. 'Ashita mo' means 'Tomorrow too'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: しゅうまつは どう でしたか。きのう、おんせんへ いきました。とても たのしかった です。あしたも いきたい です。
